title: Companies Pay Record Dividends in 2022 Despite Dismal Year
text: S&P 500 stocks paid $565 billion in 2022, up 10% from 2021

title: Tesla is not alone: 18 (and a half) other big stocks are headed for their worst year on record
text: In the worst year for stocks since the Great Recession, several big names are headed for their worst year on record with just one trading day left in 2022.

title: Carrier (CARR) Boosts Fire & Security Unit With TruVision TVN12
text: Carrier's (CARR) Aritech unveils a video surveillance recorder, namely the TruVision TVN12, which is integrated into the UltraSync cloud network.

title: Carrier Global: Good Growth Prospects
text: SEER2 and the inflation reduction act should provide a revenue tailwind for CARR in the short to medium term. Secular trends such as health and wellness, sustainability, digitalization, and the growing middle-class population should help CARR in growing its total addressable market.

title: Carrier (CARR), AWS Partner for SaaS Digital Solutions
text: Carrier (CARR) enters an agreement with AWS to offer SaaS solutions on the AWS marketplace.

title: Is the Options Market Predicting a Spike in Carrier (CARR) Stock?
text: Investors need to pay close attention to Carrier (CARR) stock based on the movements in the options market lately.

title: Delta Air Lines stock jumps on raised guidance, as carrier cites ‘robust' demand for air travel
text: Delta Air Lines' stock rose 4.7% before market open on Wednesday after the company raised its earnings guidance.

title: Paccar, Deere, and 10 More Companies That Raised Their Stock Dividends
text: Southwest Airlines also said it will resume paying a dividend.

title: Carrier Board of Directors Increases Quarterly Dividend by 23%
text: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. , Dec. 7, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Carrier Global Corporation (NYSE: CARR), the leading global provider of healthy, safe, sustainable and intelligent building and cold chain solutions, announced today that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.185 per outstanding share of Carrier common stock, which represents a 23% increase over the prior quarterly dividend.
